libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use a local fake/stub ChatModel bean so tests do not call an external AI provider or require API keys. For version 2.0.0, do not use the removed RC2 API/property around stream tool-call responses; ChatClientBuilderProperties.ToolCalling exposes enabled and advisorOrder, while observation properties expose logPrompt and logCompletion. No Docker image or environment variable is indicated for baseline coverage.

deterministic_setup.reason: Provides the ChatClient, ChatModel, advisor, observation, prompt, and response APIs referenced by the auto-configuration and needed to exercise normal ChatClient.Builder behavior.
Provides ToolCallingAutoConfiguration and tool-calling model APIs used by ChatClientAutoConfiguration's tool-calling advisor path.
Provides the Spring Boot auto-configuration annotations, conditions, and configuration-properties infrastructure used by the artifact.
Provides ApplicationContextRunner, the normal lightweight way to exercise Spring Boot auto-configuration behavior in tests.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8744 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-chat-client:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Existing 2.0.0-RC2 tests reference isStreamToolCallResponses(), which is absent in 2.0.0 and caused the reported javac failure.

The tracer-present observation branch is conditional on a Micrometer Tracer bean; baseline meaningful coverage can use the no-tracer branch, but tests deliberately targeting tracer wrapping may need additional test scaffolding.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The artifact is a Spring Boot auto-configuration module whose functional classes depend on optional Spring AI and Spring Boot APIs that are not pulled by the target artifact alone; meaningful tests need those APIs on the test classpath.
